///\n174\t    /// # Returns\n175\t    ///\n176\t    /// `true` if we're at the root scope, `false` otherwise\n177\t    pub fn is_at_root(&self) -> bool {\n178\t        self.scope_depth == 1\n179\t a specific depth\n210\t    ///\n211\t    /// # Arguments\n212\t    ///\n213\t    /// * `depth_offset` - How many levels up to go (1 = immediate parent, 2 = grandparent, etc.)\n214\t    ///\n215\t    /// # Returns\n216\t    ///\n217\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a parent exists at that level, `None` otherwise\n218\t    ///\n219\t    /// # Examples\n220\t    ///\n221\t    /// ```\n222\t    /// let depth_offset: usize) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n226\t        if self.scope_stack.scopes.is_empty() || depth_offset == 0 {\n227\t            None\n228\t        } else if depth_offset >= self.scope_stack.scopes.len() {\n229\t            None\n230\t        } else {\n231\t            self.scope_stack.scopes.get(self.scope_stack.scopes.len() - 1 - depth_offset)\n232\t        }\n233\t    }\n234\t\n235\t    /// Get the immediate parent scope (one level up)\n236\t    pub fn immediate_parent_scope(&self) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n237\t        self.parent_scope(1)\n238\t    }\n239\t\n240\t    /// Get the scope hierarchy path as a string\n241\t    ///\n242\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n243\t    /// called when entering a new scope to track its type and depth.\n269\t    /// The scope info stack provides lightweight metadata about each scope level\n270\t    /// without duplicating the full scope state.\n271\t    ///\n272\t    /// # Arguments\n273\t    ///\n274\t    /// * `scope_info` - The scope information to push onto the stack\n275\t    ///\n276\t    /// # Examples\n277\t    ///\n278\t    /// ```\n279\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{ScopeInfo, ScopeType};\n280\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::parser::BasicParser;\n281\t    ///\n282\t    /// let mut parser = fn push_scope(&mut self, scope_info: ScopeInfo) {\n288\t        self.scope_info_stack.push(scope_info);\n289\t    }\n290\t\n291\t    /// Pop scope information from the scope info stack\n292\t    ///\n293\t    /// This method is called when exiting a scope to remove its type and depth\n294\t    /// from the tracking stack. Returns None if the stack is empty.\n295\t    ///\n296\t    /// # Returns\n297\t    ///\n298\t    /// `Some(ScopeInfo)` if there was a scope to pop, `None` if the stack is empty\n299\t    ///\n300\t    /// # Examples\n301\t    ///\n302\t    /// ```\n303\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{ScopeInfo, ScopeType};\n304\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::parser::BasicParser;\n305\t    ///\n306\t    /// let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n307\t    /// let info = ScopeInfo::block(1);\n308\t    /// parser.push_scope(info);\n309\t    ///\n310\t    /// let popped = parser.pop_scope();\n311\t    /// assert!(popped.is_some());\n312\t    /// assert_eq!(popped.unwrap().scope_depth(), 1);\n313\t    ///\n314\t    /// // Popping from empty stack returns None\n315\t    /// assert!(parser.pop_scope().is_none());\n316\t    /// ```\n317\t    pub fn pop_scope(&mut self) -> Option<ScopeInfo> {\n318\t        self.scope_info_stack.pop()\n319\t    }\n320\t\n321\t    /// Update scope depth to match the current scope stack state\n322\t    ///\n323\t    /// This should be called after operations that modify the scope stack\n324\t    /// to keep the scope_depth field in sync.\n325\t    fn update_scope_depth(&mut self) {\n326\t        self.scope_depth = self.scope_stack.depth();\n327\t    }\n328\t\n329\t    /// Get all indent transitions from parsing\n330\t    ///\n331\t    /// This requires re-parsing to collect transitions. For more efficient\n332\t    /// access, use the scope stack directly during validation.\n333\t    /// Updates parser state with line classifications and transition tracking.\n334\t    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<crate::parsers::yaml::scope::IndentTransition> {\n335\t        let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n336\t\n337\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n338\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n339\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n340\t\n341\t            // Skip document markers and directives\n342\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" || trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n343\t                // Update state to track that we're on an empty line (marker line)\n344\t                self.current_line_type = LineClassification::Empty;\n345\t                continue;\n346\t            }\n347\t\n348\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n349\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n350\t\n351\t            // Track line type in parser state\n352\t            self.current_line_type = line_type;\n353\t\n354\t            // Only track actual indent changes\n355\t            if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n356\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n357\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n358\t\n359\t                // Update transition state to track this indent change\n360\t                let from_indent = scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n361\t                self.current_transition_state.update(from_indent, indent, has_key);\n362\t            }\n363\t        }\n364\t\n365\t        scope_stack.get_indent_transitions().to_vec()\n366\t    }\n367\t\n368\t    /// Get transition counts by type\n369\t    ///\n370\t    /// Returns (enter_scope_count, exit_scope_count, same_level_count)\n371\t    /// Updates parser state with line classifications during counting.\n372\t    pub fn get_transition_counts(&mut self, content: &str) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n373\t        let transitions = self.get_indent_transitions(content);\n374\t\n375\t        let enter = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_enter_scope()).count();\n376\t        let exit = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_exit_scope()).count();\n377\t        let same = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_same_level()).count();\n378\t\n379\t        (enter, exit, same)\n380\t    }\n381\t\n382\t    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n383\t    ///\n384\t    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n385\t    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n386\t    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n387\t    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&mut self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n388\t        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n389\t\n390\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n391\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n392\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n393\t\n394\t            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n395\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n396\t\n397\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n398\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n399\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n400\t                {\n401\t                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n402\t                }\n403\t                scope_stack.reset();\n404\t                continue;\n405\t            }\n406\t\n407\t            // Skip YAML directives\n408\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n409\t                continue;\n410\t            }\n411\t\n412\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n413\t\n414\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n415\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n416\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n417\t            if indent_changed {\n418\t                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n419\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n420\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n421\t            }\n422\t\n423\t            // Handle blank lines, comments, and indent-only lines with consistent logic\n424\t            // These lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n425\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n426\t            // Comments are also transparent to scope tracking since YAML parsers ignore them.\n427\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n428\t                // Update last_indent tracking so the next line can detect indent changes\n429\t                // but don't trigger any scope transitions\n430\t                if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n431\t                    scope_stack.set_last_indent(indent);\n432\t                }\n433\t                continue;\n434\t            }\n435\t\n436\t            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n437\t            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but DO trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n438\t            // This ensures proper scope cleanup when returning to outer levels through blank lines\n439\t            // CRITICAL: Scope stack must exit on indent decrease even without keys to maintain consistency\n440\t            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n441\t                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n442\t                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n443\t                    // Record the indent transition with has_key=false for indent-only lines\n444\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, false, line);\n445\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n446\t                } else if indent > scope_stack.current_indent() {\n447\t                    // Indent increased on indent-only line - just record the transition, don't enter scope\n448\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, false, line);\n449\t                }\n450\t                continue;\n451\t            }\n452\t\n453\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n454\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n455\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n456\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n457\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n458\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n459\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n460\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n461\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n462\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n463\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n464\t                                    dup_err.message()\n465\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n466\t                            }\n467\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n468\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n469\t                            {\n470\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n471\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n472\t                            }\n473\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n474\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n475\t                                line_num_1index,\n476\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n477\t                            );\n478\t                            // Track scope info on the parser's scope info stack\n479\t                            let scope_info = ScopeInfo::block(scope_stack.depth());\n480\t                            self.push_scope(scope_info);\n481\t                        } else {\n482\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n483\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n484\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n485\t                            {\n486\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n487\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n488\t                            }\n489\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n490\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n491\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n492\t                                    dup_err.message()\n493\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n494\t                            }\n495\t                        }\n496\t                    } else {\n497\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n498\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n499\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n500\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n501\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n502\t                        {\n503\t                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={},        }\n507\t                }\n508\t                Ordering::Less => {\n509\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n510\t                    // Check if we're transitioning to a sequence item at same level as parent\n511\t                    // In this case, we should NOT exit the parent scope\n512\t                    let trimmed = line.trim();\n513\t                    let is_sequence_item_at_parent_level = trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") &&\n514\t                        scope_stack.current_indent() == indent + scope_stack.base_indent();\n515\t\n516\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n517\t                    {\n518\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n519\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}', is_sequence_item_at_parent_level={}\",\n520\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path, is_sequence_item_at_parent_level);\n521\t                    }\n522\t\n523\t                    if !is_sequence_item_at_parent_level {\n524\t                        scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n525\t                    }\n526\t\n527\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n528\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n529\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n530\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n531\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n532\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n533\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n534\t                                    dup_err.message()\n535\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n536\t                            }\n537\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n538\t                            {\n539\t                         line_num_1index,\n545\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n546\t                            );\n547\t                            // Track scope                     } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n551\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n552\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n553\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n554\t                                    dup_err.message()\n555\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n556\t                            }\n557\t                        }\n558\t                    }\n559\t                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n560\t                }\n561\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n562\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n563\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n564\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n565\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent                         line_num_1index,\n588\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n589\t                            );\n590\t                            // Track scope                     } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n594\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n595\t                          ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n599\t                            }\n600\t                        }\n601\t                    }\n602\t                    // Edge case: same self.push_scope(scope_info);\n619\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n620\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) self.push_scope(scope_info);\n637\t                }\n638\t            }\n639\t        }\n640\t\n641\t        duplicate_errors\n642\t    }\n643\t}\n644\t\n645\timpl Default for BasicParser {\n646\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n813\t                                    dup_err.message()\n814\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n815\t                            }\n816\t                        }\n817\t                    }\n818\t                }\n819\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n820\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n821\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n822\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n823\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n824\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n825\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n826\t                            {\n827\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n828\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n829\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n830\t                            }\n831\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n832\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n833\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n834\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n835\t                                    dup_err.message()\n836\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n837\t                            }\n838\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n839\t                            {\n840\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n841\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n842\t                            }\n843\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n844\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n845\t                                line_num_1index,\n846\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n847\t                            );\n848\t                            // Track scope info on the parser's scope info stack\n849\t                            let scope_info = ScopeInfo::block(scope_stack.depth());\n850\t                            self.push_scope(scope_info);\n851\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n852\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n853\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n854\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n855\t                                    dup_err.message()\n856\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n857\t                            }\n858\t                        }\n859\t                    }\n860\t                }\n861\t            }\n862\t\n863\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n864\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n865\t                // When starting a new sequence item, exit any existing sequence scope at the same indent\n866\t                if scope_stack.current_indent() == indent && scope_stack.in_sequence_context() {\n867\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n868\t                    {\n869\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Exiting previous sequence scope before entering new one at indent={}, line={}\",\n870\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n871\t                    }\n872\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent.saturating_sub(1));\n873\t                }\n874\t\n875\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n876\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n877\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n878\t                    {\n879\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n880\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n881\t                    }\n882\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n883\t                    // Track scope info on the parser's scope info stack\n884\t                    let scope_info = ScopeInfo::new(ScopeType::BlockSequence, scope_stack.depth());\n885\t                    self.push_scope(scope_info);\n886\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n887\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n888\t                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n889\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n890\t                            dup_err.message()\n891\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n892\t                    }\n893\t                } else {\n894\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n895\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n896\t                    {\n897\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n898\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n899\t                    }\n900\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n901\t                    // Track scope info on the parser's scope info stack\n902\t                    let scope_info = ScopeInfo::new(ScopeType::BlockSequence, scope_stack.depth());\n903\t                    self.push_scope(scope_info);\n904\t                }\n905\t            }\n906\t        }\n907\t\n908\t        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n909\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n910\t            Ok(value) => {\n911\t                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n912\t                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n913\t                ParseResult::success(value)\n914\t            }\n915\t            Err(err) => {\n916\t                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n917\t                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n918\t                    err.to_string()\n919\t                } else {\n920\t                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n921\t                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n922\t                };\n923\t                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n924\t            }\n925\t        }\n926\t    }\n927\t\n928\t    fn parse_bytes(&mut self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n929\t        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n930\t        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n931\t            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n932\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n933\t        }\n934\t    }\n935\t\n936\t    fn parse_file(&mut self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n937\t        // Read file content\n938\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n939\t            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n940\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n941\t        }\n942\t    }\n943\t\n944\t    fn validate_str(&mut self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n945\t        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n946\t        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n947\t            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n948\t        } else {\n949\t            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n950\t        };\n951\t\n952\t        // Run syntax validation\n953\t        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n954\t\n955\t        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n956\t        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n957\t            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n958\t            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n959\t            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n960\t                result.valid = false;\n961\t                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n962\t            }\n963\t        }\n964\t\n965\t        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n966\t        if result.is_valid() {\n967\t            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n968\t            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n969\t\n970\t            // Merge errors from detector\n971\t            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n972\t                result.valid = false;\n973\t                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n974\t            }\n975\t        }\n976\t\n977\t        result\n978\t    }\n979\t\n980\t    fn validate_file(&mut self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n981\t        // Read file content\n982\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n983\t            Ok(content) => content,\n984\t            Err(err) => {\n985\t                return ValidationResult {\n986\t                    valid: false,\n987\t                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n988\t                        path.display().to_string(),\n989\t                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n990\t                    )],\n991\t                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n992\t                };\n993\t            }\n994\t        };\n995\t\n996\t        // Validate the content\n997\t        self.validate_str(&content)\n998\t    }\n999\t\n1000\t    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n1001\t        &self.config\n1002\t    }\n1003\t\n1004\t    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n1005\t    where\n1006\t        Self: Sized,\n1007\t    {\n1008\t        Self {\n1009\t            config,\n1010\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n1011\t            scope_info_stack: Vec::new(),    // Empty stack initially\n1012\t            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n1013\t            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n1014\t            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n1015\t        }\n1016\t    }\n1017\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t// ============================================================================\n1020\t// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n1021\t// ============================================================================\n1022\t\n1023\t#[cfg(test)]\n1024\tmod integration_tests {\n1025\t    use super::*;\n1026\t\n1027\t    /// Test push_scope adds scope info to stack\n1028\t    #[test]\n1029\t    fn test_push_scope() {\n1030\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n1031\t\n1032\t        // Initially scope_info_stack should be empty\n1033\t        assert_eq!(parser.scope_info_stack().len(), 0, \"Initial scope info stack should be empty\");\n1034\t\n1035\t        // Create a scope info and push it\n1036\t        let scope_info = ScopeInfo::block(1);\n1037\t        parser.push_scope(scope_info);\n1038\t\n1039\t        // Verify it was added\n1040\t        assert_eq!(parser.scope_info_stack().len(), 1, \"Scope info stack should have 1 item after push\");\n1041\t\n1042\t        // Verify the pushed scope info matches\n1043\t        let pushed_info = parser.scope_info_stack().last().unwrap();\n1044\t        assert_eq!(pushed_info.scope_type(), ScopeType::Block, \"Pushed scope should be Block type\");\n1045\t        assert_eq!(pushed_info.scope_depth(), 1, \"Pushed scope should have depth 1\");\n1046\t    }\n1047\t\n1048\t    /// Test push_scope multiple times\n1049\t    #[test]\n1050\t    fn test_push_scope_multiple() {\n1051\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n1052\t\n1053\t        // Push multiple scopes\n1054\t        parser.push_scope(ScopeInfo::block(1));\n1055\t        parser.push_scope(ScopeInfo::block(2));\n1056\t        parser.push_scope(ScopeInfo::block(3));\n1057\t\n1058\t        // Verify all were added\n1059\t        assert_eq!(parser.scope_info_stack().len(), 3, \"Scope info stack should have 3 items\");\n1060\t\n1061\t        // Verify they're in order\n1062\t test_transition_type_classification() {\n1873\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n1874\t\n1875\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1876\tkey1: value1\n1877\t  nested:\n1878\t    deep:        for transition in &transitions {\n1886\t            let transition_type = transition.transition_type();\n1887\t\n1888\t            // Verify classification matches indent change\n1889\t            if transition.to_indent > transition.from_indent {\n1890\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::EnterScope,\n1891\t                           \"Indent increase should be classified as EnterScope\");\n1892\t            } else if transition.to_indent < transition.from_indent {\n1893\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::ExitScope,\n1894\t                           \"Indent decrease should be classified as ExitScope\");\n1895\t            } else {\n1896\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::SameLevel,\n1897\t                           \"No indent change should be classified as SameLevel\");\n1898\t            }\n1899\t        }\n1900\t    }\n1901\t\n1902\t    /// Test scope operation mapping\n1903\t    #[test]\n1904\t    fn test_scope_operation_mapping() {\n1905\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n1906\t\n1907\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1908\tparent:\n1909\t  child: value\n1910\tsibling:
